north_star: Turn one uploaded book into a clean, organized, evidence-backed learning engine with a knowledge graph.
hard_rules: Do not optimize for a chatbot before the extracted book is clean.
Do not let retrieval hide extraction failures.
Do not silently drop PDF content.
Do not treat LLM output as ground truth without provenance and review.
Do not widen to many books before one book passes Phase 1 gates.
Do not spend expensive model calls without recording why the model was chosen.
Do not commit, document, or log API keys.
Do not choose OpenAI models silently; model choice must be explicit before model-assisted extraction or review.
Do not use Markdown as the durable project documentation format, except README.md as a GitHub-facing adapter.
Do not call Phase 1 outputs canonical until they pass audit, comparison, and review.
Do not hide review corrections inside detector logic; store durable corrections as tracked review override artifacts under reviews/.
Stage, commit, and push meaningful checkpoints when the repo state should be preserved.

phase1_candidate_first_rule.rule: No Phase 1 output should be called canonical until it has passed an audit step.

canonical_promotion_rule.meaning: Canonical means evidence-bound, audited enough under current rules, and safe for downstream use; it does not mean flawless.
canonical_promotion_rule.review_gate: Review canonical_paragraphs.jsonl with canonical_paragraph_review_report.json before downstream use, including warning drilldowns, risk clusters, bbox span diagnostics and decision summaries when span risk dominates, merge experiment reports before adopting merge policy changes, and top corrective recommendation.

start: Read current Phase 1 strategy and artifact contract.
Read the latest relevant history entry.
Append an observability event at the start of meaningful work, before the first edit.
closure: Run python3 -m pytest tests before committing code or artifact-contract changes.
If pytest is unavailable, use the direct test-function runner only as a temporary fallback and document the limitation.
Update history, lessons, or strategy when the result changes project knowledge.
Append an observability event summarizing the outcome.
Stage, commit, and push meaningful durable changes.

verification.fallback_policy: Direct test-function execution is allowed only as a temporary fallback when pytest is unavailable; document the fallback.

formatting_rules.local_html_docs: Use the standard light document style in docs/.
formatting_rules.dark_mode_scope: Reserve dark mode for runtime observability surfaces, not ordinary project documentation.
formatting_rules.github_readme_scope: README.md is a GitHub-facing adapter, not the durable local source of truth.

observability.port_rule: Reuse the same observability port. Do not open new ports for each build.
